note: This verification pass was performed directly by the session orchestrator (not the cdr-verification subagent) because the dispatched cdr-verification agent (a56adf939bc18fb17) was terminated mid-task by an account session-limit error before producing a verdict. The orchestrator independently read the full test source and independently re-ran all validation commands below rather than trusting the interrupted agent's partial transcript.
acceptance_criteria: Full concurrent race-test suite: mixed insert/delete/lookup workload across disjoint and overlapping subtrees, oracle-matching final state, zero race-detector reports. Test spec: go test ./engine/btree/... -race -run TestConcurrentMixedWorkload -count=5.

test_design_review.roles: TestConcurrentMixedWorkload runs 15 insertOnly goroutines (disjoint modulo-slice over key range [0,3000)), 15 deleteOnly goroutines (disjoint modulo-slice over a pre-seeded [3000,6000)), 10 mutate goroutines (disjoint blocks over [6000,8000), each doing delete-then-reinsert to force repeated local split/merge churn), and 10 lookup goroutines running continuously across the ENTIRE keyspace [0,totalKeys) until all writers finish.
test_design_review.disjoint_vs_overlapping: Write roles operate on mutually disjoint key ranges (a deliberate, necessary choice to keep a serial-execution oracle well-defined per this subtask's own design guidance -- a free-for-all same-key race across operation types would make 'correct final state' ambiguous by construction, not a stronger test). 'Overlapping subtrees' is satisfied structurally rather than at the leaf-key level: with 8,000 sequentially-keyed entries stored in a single shared B+Tree, insertOnly/deleteOnly/mutate/lookup all share common ancestor internal nodes (especially near the root) despite disjoint leaf-level key ranges -- consistent with how 'OverlappingSubtree' was already defined and tested in 2a.4.2's own TestCrabbingInsert/OverlappingSubtree precedent. Lookups explicitly read across ALL three write ranges concurrently with their mutation, which is where the genuine read/write overlap this subtask exists to test actually occurs.
test_design_review.oracle: A per-key validFileIDs map is precomputed BEFORE any goroutine runs (insertOnly keys map to exactly one valid fileID; mutate-range keys map to either their v0 or v1 fileID, since a concurrent reader could legitimately observe either mid-mutation). Lookup goroutines check every found result against this oracle and fail the test immediately on any corrupted/unexpected fileID. Post-workload, final state is checked for: (a) every insertOnly/mutate key present with correct fileID (cross-checked, per handoff notes, against both Tree.Lookup and the independent Phase-1 free Lookup), (b) every deleteOnly key absent, (c) assertStructuralInvariants and assertNoOrphanedPointers hold on the final tree.
test_design_review.companion_test: TestConcurrentMixedWorkloadForcedLookupDuringDelete deterministically forces the single highest-risk interleaving (an optimistic Tree.Lookup read racing a concurrent Tree.Delete's structural mutation) via the same hook-based synchronization pattern established in TestOptimisticRead/ForcedRetryDeterministic (2a.4.4) -- confirmed passing.
test_design_review.scale_assessment: Scale was reduced from an original 80k-key/200-goroutine configuration to 8k keys/50 goroutines during the 013-implementation-fix cycle, after the original scale was found to reliably hit an unrelated pre-existing retry-backoff/contention throughput ceiling under a 20m timeout (goroutine dumps showed heavy runnable/semacquire churn among 200 goroutines on 8 CPUs, not a cyclic-wait deadlock) -- already independently corroborated once in 014-verification (original scale confirmed to still pass cleanly under -race in ~121s when run alone). At 4096-byte NodeSize with these key/fileID sizes, a leaf node holds on the order of ~100+ entries, so 8,000 keys still produces dozens of leaves and multiple levels of internal nodes, and the mutate role's repeated delete+reinsert on its own 200-key-per-goroutine block is sufficient to force repeated local splits and merges during the run. This is judged adequate to satisfy the subtask's structural-coverage intent, though it is a genuinely smaller scale than 2a.4.2's own largest stress test (VeryDeepOverlappingSubtree, 80k keys) -- noted as a non-blocking comment, not a defect, since the reduction was scale-throughput-motivated and independently corroborated rather than a silent weakening to hide a bug.

validation_independently_rerun.acceptance_spec: PASS 5/5 -- go test ./btree/... -race -run TestConcurrentMixedWorkload -count=5 -timeout 25m -v: all 5 TestConcurrentMixedWorkload runs and all 5 TestConcurrentMixedWorkloadForcedLookupDuringDelete runs PASS, ~15.9s total wall time
validation_independently_rerun.full_btree_suite: PASS -- go test ./btree/... -race -v -count=1 -timeout 10m: all tests green (TestInsertSplit, TestInsertOutOfOrder, TestCrabbingInsert incl. VeryDeepOverlappingSubtree, TestCrabbingConcurrentPropagateNoDeadlock, TestLookup, TestOptimisticRead incl. ForcedRetryDeterministic, TestNodeSerialization, TestNodeLatchFields, TestPrefixScan variants), 44.1s wall time, zero regressions across Phase-1 through 2a.4.4

task_2a4_closure_assessment: All 5 subtasks of task-2a.4 (2a.4.1 per-node latch/version, 2a.4.2 crabbing insert, 2a.4.3 crabbing delete, 2a.4.4 lock-free optimistic read, 2a.4.5 this mixed-workload capstone) are now implemented, fixed where needed, and verified. The capstone test fulfilled its purpose: it caught 3 genuine cross-subtask interaction bugs (2a.4.2 x 2a.4.3, 2a.4.3 x 2a.4.4) that no single-operation-type stress test could have found, and those bugs are now fixed and independently re-verified (014-verification, PASS_WITH_COMMENTS) at the shared insert/delete/lookup surface. GitHub issue #9 (task-2a.4) is judged CLOSABLE.
non_blocking_followups_carried_forward: No retry cap on the TryLock restart-from-root loop (tracked since 2a.4.2, .cdr/memory/pending.md) -- reinforced by fresh evidence from the 80k/200-goroutine throughput-ceiling investigation during the fix cycle; still open, still low observed risk at production-realistic scales.
Node-latch registry has no eviction (tracked since 2a.4.1) -- still open.
F1: Tree.Lookup's doc comment slightly overclaims 'never locks' (it briefly acquires rootMu via t.Root()) -- tracked since 2a.4.4; still open, doc-scoping only.

Bugs 2 and 3 from the 013-implementation-fix cycle (repairEmptyLeafAtParent orphaning, split write-ordering) have no dedicated fast regression test of their own beyond the large probabilistic capstone test -- worth a follow-up fast/deterministic regression test at some point, not blocking.
